content.full_text:
35-day statement 
led in connection with 
lification of initiative 
A statement filed in connection with Section 84202 is not a post-
election statement and therefore does not terminate one's obli-
gation to cumulate.

sections.conclusion: A statement filed in connection with Section 84202 is not a post-election statement and therefore does not terminate one's obligation to cumulate.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a statement filed under Section 84202 in connection with initiative qualification constitutes a post-election statement that would terminate cumulation obligations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that a Section 84202 statement filed for initiative qualification does not qualify as a post-election statement and thus does not relieve the filer from cumulation obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be a fragment or excerpt with minimal context. No formal question or conclusion sections are clearly delineated. The text is brief and lacks standard advice letter formatting with explicit Q&A sections. Document ID and year indicate this is from 1979.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a statement filed under Section 84202 in connection with initiative qualification constitutes a post-election statement that would terminate cumulation obligations.

CONCLUSION: A statement filed in connection with Section 84202 is not a post-election statement and therefore does not terminate one's obligation to cumulate.
embedding.first_500_words: 35-day statement led in connection with lification of initiative A statement filed in connection with Section 84202 is not a post- election statement and therefore does not terminate one's obli- gation to cumulate.
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter 79-196 addresses whether statements filed under Section 84202 in connection with initiative qualification terminate cumulation obligations, concluding they do not constitute post-election statements.
